articles.headline: GAA Names 2026 National Honorees

articles.headline: UK Aviation Explodes: 31 Flight Routes Hit 1 Million Passengers In 2025
articles.blurb: The UK's three largest airports had 31 routes with 1 million+ passengers. But 2026 will bring a fourth airport to the list.
articles.takeaway: UK's fourth major airport reaching 1M+ passenger routes in 2026 signals concentrated growth consolidation among hub airports, challenging regional connectivity.

articles.headline: How Russia Defied Sanctions To Maintain Its Long-Haul Commercial Fleet
articles.blurb: In the wake of 2022 sanctions, Russia defied predictions of a grounded fleet by building a parallel, self-sustaining ecosystem to stay flying.
articles.takeaway: Russia's adaptive supply chain strategy demonstrates how geopolitical sanctions can paradoxically strengthen domestic aviation capability through forced vertical integration.

articles.headline: NASA Simulations Improve Artemis II Launch Environment
articles.blurb: Airflow around rockets as they travel from Earth into space can have a dramatic impact on a mission, which is why NASA used advanced simulations to provide the best possible launch conditions for the Artemis II test flight around the Moon. To better understand the Artemis Space Launch System (SLS...
articles.takeaway: Advanced computational fluid dynamics simulations optimize launch environments, reducing mission risk and improving trajectory precision for heavy-lift vehicles.

articles.headline: Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

articles.takeaway: Effective safety risk management requires organizations to systematically select controls and formally document acceptance of residual risks to meet regulatory compliance standards.

articles.blurb: From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.
articles.takeaway: Modern SMS transforms aviation safety from reactive incident investigation to proactive risk management, fundamentally changing how operators prevent accidents.

As you may or may not already know, the 767-based tanker transport has experi...

articles.headline: How Iran Secretly Built a Boeing Fleet Under Sanctions
articles.blurb: For decades, Iran has operated one of the most unusual airline fleets in the world. Despite sweeping international sanctions designed to isolate the country from the global aerospace industry, Iranian airlines still fly dozens of Boeing and Airbus aircraft. Many of those jets were never supposed ...
articles.takeaway: Iran's covert aircraft acquisition network reveals critical vulnerabilities in sanctions enforcement and supply chain controls for commercial aviation assets.

articles.headline: Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization
articles.blurb: Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.
articles.takeaway: Operational optimization through advanced technologies is critical to managing surging air traffic while meeting environmental sustainability demands across aviation systems.
keywords: aviation; optimization; operations research; sustainability; environmental impacts
